# Purse Seine 

Purse sine fisheries data is to be stratified by fishing mode (e.g. free-swimming schools or schools associated with floating objects). The E-SET section of the reporting form includes data fields to reporting the sighting cues for the set, school types, and the detection method.  Activities of Fish Aggregating Devices (FADs) are an integral part of purse seine operations, FAD data including the FAD identifier, type, and design characteristics can be reported on the E-SET sheet.  

CMM for the conservation of cetacean’s states that the assessment of the life status of the individual upon release must be reported, included whether the cetacean was released alive but then died. These data fields can be found in the E-SET-CATCHES-SPECIMENS-SSI section. A description of the interaction is to be included in the trip report including the location, how and why it occurred, the steps taken to release the individual, and an assessment of the condition of the individual.
